user_query:
I need a quick, defensible short-list of low-risk textile suppliers for our Q1 2024 pilot. Please use the local inputs in input/ to compute a Sustainability Risk Index (SRI) per supplier and produce three deliverables:

1) output/supplier_scores.csv — one row per supplier, sorted by final_score ascending, with columns:
- supplier_id
- supplier_name
- region
- total_purchase_kg_q1 (sum of kg for 2024-01, 2024-02, 2024-03)
- total_emissions_kgco2e_q1 (sum over purchases of kg * emission_factor for the category)
- audit_severity (from audit_findings.jsonl)
- open_findings (from audit_findings.jsonl)
- audit_subscore_raw (see below)
- audit_subscore_norm (min–max normalized across suppliers; if max=min, set all to 0)
- carbon_subscore_norm (min–max normalized from total_emissions_kgco2e_q1; if max=min, set all to 0)
- volume_subscore_norm (min–max normalized from total_purchase_kg_q1; if max=min, set all to 0)
- region_multiplier (from policy)
- final_score
- shortlisted (true/false)
- flag_reasons (comma-separated reasons if not shortlisted; use any of: critical_audit, too_many_open_findings, final_score_above_threshold)

2) output/shortlist.json — array of objects [{ supplier_id, supplier_name, region, final_score }] for suppliers that meet the shortlisting rule below, sorted by final_score ascending.

3) output/summary_email.txt — a draft email to procurement@org.example with:
- a clear subject mentioning "Q1 2024 Supplier SRI Shortlist"
- a short method overview referencing policy assumptions used
- the top 3 lowest-risk suppliers with final_score and region
- a sentence noting any suppliers excluded for critical audits or excessive open findings
- a brief note on where to find the attached outputs (the two files above)

Data and rules:
- Read all inputs from input/.
- Use only Q1 2024 purchases (2024-01 to 2024-03) in input/purchases.csv.
- Emission factors, weights, region multipliers, severity map, and thresholds are defined in input/policy.yaml.
- For each supplier:
  • total_purchase_kg_q1 = sum of kg for Q1 2024.
  • total_emissions_kgco2e_q1 = sum over Q1 rows of (kg * emission_factors[category]).
  • audit_subscore_raw = severity_map[severity] * (1 + open_findings/10.0).
  • Compute three min–max normalized subscores across all suppliers present: audit_subscore_norm, carbon_subscore_norm (from total_emissions_kgco2e_q1), volume_subscore_norm (from total_purchase_kg_q1). If a subscore has max == min, set all normalized values for that subscore to 0.
  • final_score = (weights.audit * audit_subscore_norm + weights.carbon * carbon_subscore_norm + weights.volume * volume_subscore_norm) * region_multipliers[region].
- Shortlisting rule: A supplier is shortlisted if (final_score <= shortlist_threshold) AND (exclude_if_critical is false OR audit_severity != 'critical') AND (open_findings <= exclude_if_open_findings_gt).

Please ensure all three outputs are created exactly at the specified paths under output/. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
